data.title: Telenor ASA reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Telenor ASA press release (TELNY): Q1 Revenue of NOK 14.8B.

    • Adjusted EBITDA of NOK 8.0 billion.

    • Free cash flow before M&A of NOK 2.1 billion.

    • Total free cash flow of 31.9 bn NOK.

    Service revenues increased by 1.6 per cent compared to the same period last year, while adjusted EBITDA grew by 3.1 per cent, both organically. The results reflect slower growth in the Nordics compared to 2025, especially in Finland, combined with a challenging situation in Bangladesh.

data.title: Anthropic teams up with Adobe, others to use Claude for creative work
data.description:
Anthropic (ANTHRO) and its partners, including Blender, Autodesk (ADSK), Adobe (ADBE), Ableton, and Splice, are releasing a set of connectors for Claude to access their platforms and tools directly for creative work.

The connectors include: Ableton grounds Claude’s answers in official product documentation for Live and Push; Adobe for creativity enables users to bring images, videos, and designs to life, drawing from over 50 tools across Creative Cloud apps including Photoshop, Premiere, Express, and more.

In addition, Affinity by Canva automates repetitive production tasks across pro creative workflows — such as batch image adjustments, layer renaming, and file export — and generates custom features directly in the app.

Autodesk Fusion allows designers and engineers with a Fusion subscription to create and modify 3D models through conversations with Claude. Meanwhile, Resolume Arena and Resolume Wire let video jockeys, or VJs, and live visual artists control Arena, Avenue, and Wire in real time through natural language for live performance.

SketchUp will turn a conversation with Claude into a starting point for 3D modeling. In addition, Splice gives music producers the ability to search its catalog of royalty-free samples from within Claude, according to the company.

The company said Claude can act as an on-demand tutor for complex software. A user can ask it to explain a modifier stack, walk them through a synthesis technique, or demonstrate an unfamiliar feature, and it will show them how to use it.

Anthropic noted that Blender developers have created a Model Context Protocol, or MCP, connector, which is now officially available for Claude. 

Anthropic added that it is also working with art and design programs to support curricula that involve creative computation. The first three such programs are Art and Computation at Rhode Island School of Design, Fundamentals of AI for Creatives at Ringling College of Art and Design, and the MA/MFA Computational Arts program at Goldsmiths, University of London.

Students and faculty will get access to Claude and the new connectors and provide feedback to the company.

data.title: Centene raises 2026 adjusted EPS outlook to greater than $3.40 as it embeds around 3% Marketplace pretax margin
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Centene Corporation (CNC) Q1 2026

Management view

  • “This morning, we reported first quarter adjusted diluted EPS of $3.37, exceeding our previous expectations for the period,” said CEO Sarah London (CEO & Director Sarah London), adding, “The strength of our first quarter performance enables us to increase our full year 2026 adjusted EPS outlook to greater than $3.40, up from our previous expectation of greater than $3.”
  • On Medicaid execution and trend drivers, London said, “Medicaid results in the quarter were ahead of our previous projection,” while “Behavioral health remains the largest driver of trend with other categories like home health and high-cost drugs continuing to be consistent contributors,” and “we are beginning to see pockets of deceleration across this cohort.”
  • On Medicare, London said, “Our Medicare segment results were ahead of expectations with outperformance from both Medicare Advantage and PDP offerings,” and disclosed, “Both Medicare Advantage and PDP exceeded expectations, producing an HBR of 84.9%.”
  • On Marketplace/Ambetter, London said, “Marketplace results were in line for the quarter with a slightly higher HBR, offset by outperformance in SG&A,” and framed the Silver-tier dynamic: “we now view this utilization as consistent with the acuity of the Silver members we enrolled, and we expect this membership to receive a meaningful risk adjustment offset.”
  • CFO Andrew Asher (Executive VP & CFO Andrew Asher) said, “Today, we reported a strong first quarter, including $44.7 billion in Premium and Service revenue,” and added, “Cash flow provided by operations was $4.4 billion for Q1.”

Outlook

  • London said the company is “taking a prudent outlook for the balance of 2026,” even after the Q1 lift, and stated Medicaid guidance “assumes net trend… remains in the mid-4% range.”
  • Asher reiterated, “we are pleased to increase full year adjusted EPS guidance to greater than $3.40,” while keeping “no change to our HBR full year range of 90.9% to 91.7%.”
  • On Marketplace profitability assumptions inside guidance, Asher said, “We thought it would be prudent to embed in our current guidance a pretax margin for Marketplace around 3% for now compared to our original forecast of approximately 4%,” and London added, “we have taken what we believe to be a prudent posture… not reflecting the full suggested risk adjustment offset.”
  • On quarterly cadence, Asher said, “we expect a step down in earnings from Q1 to Q2, still profitable, Q3 around breakeven, and then Q4 in a loss position given the seasonality of the business.”

Financial results

  • Asher said the Q1 upside versus internal expectations was “just under $0.50 better than our expectations, largely driven by outperformance in Medicaid and Medicare segment HBRs,” and reported, “Our consolidated HBR was 87.3% for Q1.”
  • In Medicaid, Asher said, “We ended Q1 with 12.4 million members,” and “Q1 [Medicaid HBR] at 93.1%, an improvement of 50 basis points from the first quarter of 2025,” while characterizing this as “the third consecutive quarter of progress toward that goal.”
  • In Marketplace membership, Asher said, “We ended the quarter with 3.58 million members,” and guided, “ending 2026 a little over 3 million members.”
  • Balance sheet and liquidity items included, “We ended the quarter with $437 million of cash available for general corporate use,” and “the company sold $1 billion of our stand-alone 2025 Part D risk share receivables… [and] repurchase $1 billion of senior notes… [with] a debt-to-cap ratio of 43.2%, down from 46.5% at year-end.”

Q&A

  • Andrew Mok, Barclays Bank PLC, Research Division: “can you help us understand why you believe you attracted that higher acuity cohort… [and] what sort of margin would that imply” if full risk adjustment offset? CEO London: “the expiration of the enhanced APTCs… drove a shift… from silver membership into bronze,” and “the Silver tier remaining membership… when it shrinks, it becomes more morbid,” adding that the range “wrap[s] around our original 4% target margin… and frankly, higher than that at the top end.”
  • Albert Rice, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: asked if Medicaid outperformance was “primarily due to the flu and weather” vs “underlying performance.” CEO London: “A bigger piece of that was flu, a little bit of weather, but there was still fundamental solid outperformance… driven by the business,” and reiterated, “I would be disappointed if we didn’t beat 93.7%.”
  • Ann Hynes, Mizuho Securities USA LLC, Research Division: asked whether upcoming maturities would be refinanced “at higher rates.” CFO Asher: “we would look to refinance those or… part of those at least a year out,” adding, “we’re really pleased with the cash generation of the business.”
  • Erin Wilson Wright, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: asked for quarterly earnings/MLR cadence. CFO Asher: “Q2, Q3 HBRs [in Medicaid] to be higher than average,” Marketplace HBR “a steady uptick… throughout the year,” and SG&A “always the heaviest in Q4.”
  • Scott Fidel, Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., Research Division: probed LIS vs non-LIS. CFO Asher: “we’re about 1/3… LIS… about 2/3… non-low income,” and said trends “continue to be very high in non-low income… pharma taking advantage also of that $2,000 maximum out of pocket,” while adding, “we think that direct subsidy is going to go up quite a bit again as we think about 2027.”

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was slightly negative/pressing around Marketplace risk adjustment and Medicare/PDP trend mechanics, including “what sort of margin would that imply” (Mok, Barclays) and “how you have confidence that the slope will be so steep” (James, Cantor Fitzgerald).
  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks and more technical/defensive in Q&A, emphasizing prudence and partial recognition of potential upside, including “we are taking a prudent outlook” (CEO London) and “we thought it would be prudent to embed… around 3%” (CFO Asher).
  • Versus last quarter, management language shifted from establishing a baseline (“greater than $3” in Q4) to raising guidance (“greater than $3.40” in Q1) while still stressing it is “early” and maintaining a cautious posture on Marketplace risk adjustment.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Guidance increased to “greater than $3.40” (Q1) from “greater than $3” (Q4), with Q1 attributing the change to “increased visibility and operational improvements” (CEO London).
  • Marketplace framing shifted from expecting “a meaningful payable position” for 2026 risk adjustment (Q4) to forecasting “a slight receivable versus a prior payable forecast” (Q1) and embedding a lower Marketplace pretax margin assumption (“around 3%… compared to… approximately 4%”).
  • Medicare rate commentary improved in tone: Q4 cited the 2027 “advanced notice… suggests a more pressured view of rates,” while Q1 said “the finalized 2027 Medicare Advantage rates showed improvement compared to the advanced rate notice.”
  • Q&A focus remained consistent on Medicaid trend/rates and Marketplace metal-tier mix, but Q1 concentrated more on the new Wakely data process and risk adjustment receivable mechanics.

Risks and concerns

  • London highlighted continued cost pressure areas: “Behavioral health remains the largest driver of trend” with “home health and high-cost drugs” also contributing.
  • Marketplace uncertainty remained tied to risk adjustment visibility: London said, “The June Wakely data… will be key,” and Asher described differing “thought process for what we actually book in the first quarter” versus year-end forecasting.
  • Policy and program changes were a recurring topic, including Medicaid work requirements discussions; London noted Nebraska as an early mover and said states may warrant “midyear rates and retros.”

Final takeaway

Management characterized Q1 as an early-year earnings step-up driven by Medicaid and Medicare HBR outperformance, enabling Centene to raise 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to greater than $3.40 while holding a cautious posture on Marketplace profitability until additional industry data (June Wakely) refines risk adjustment assumptions. Leadership also emphasized ongoing Medicaid margin recovery levers, continued Medicare pathway toward breakeven, and balance-sheet actions that reduced leverage after selling $1 billion of Part D receivables and repurchasing $1 billion of senior notes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UAE's OPEC exit signals oil order fracture - analyst
data.description:
The United Arab Emirates on Tuesday announced its departure from OPEC, effective May 1, a decision that lands amid unprecedented turmoil in Gulf energy markets as the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted. Gregory Brew, an analyst at Eurasia Group, framed the move as part of a broader collapse in the traditional oil-security framework.

“Carter Doctrine is essentially dead, tankers are being seized/attacked on the open seas, OPEC members are breaking off to produce what they want, SPRs are being drained,” he wrote on X.

“Any and all forces that were able to produce market stability are falling by the wayside,” he added.

The UAE had long chafed at OPEC production limits as it expanded capacity and sought more room to sell crude (USO) (BNO). UAE Energy Minister Suhail Al Mazrouei said in a CNBC interview the decision followed a “very careful and long review” of the country’s policies and strategies, emphasizing that the UAE’s international investments and aspirations require greater flexibility than membership in the cartel allows.

data.title: Newegg Commerce reports FY results
data.description:
• Newegg Commerce press release (NEGG): FY Revenue of $1.44B.
  • GMV increased to $1.77 billion, compared to GMV of $1.53 billion in fiscal year 2024
  • Gross profit increased to $168.5 million, compared to gross profit of $131.5 million in fiscal year 2024
  • Net loss decreased to $4.9 million, compared to net loss of $43.3 million in fiscal year 2024
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $24.8 million, compared to Adjusted EBITDA of ($9.5) million in fiscal year 2024

data.title: Microsoft Q3 2026 Earnings Preview: Poised for another strong quarter?
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is scheduled to announce Q3 earnings results on Wednesday, April 29th, after market close.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $4.06 and the consensus revenue estimate is $81.43B (+16.2% Y/Y).

Shares are down nearly 12% YTD, but despite recent weakness in stock performance, Wall Street remains highly bullish on the tech giant.

Morgan Stanley believes Microsoft (MSFT) may look to “take back the narrative,” as investors remain cautious for several reasons.

“Microsoft exited F2Q26 with clear evidence of durable commercial demand, yet investor focus remains centered on supply constraints and GPU allocation weighing on Azure growth and increasing concerns around Copilot's competitive positioning versus AI Labs,” analyst Keith Weiss wrote in a note.

Microsoft ended the previous quarter with revenue up 17% on growth from its Intelligent Cloud division, which consists of its Azure cloud unit.

“We are only at the beginning phases of AI diffusion, and already Microsoft has built an AI business that is larger than some of our biggest franchises,” said Satya Nadella, chairman and chief executive officer of Microsoft, in a statement. “We are pushing the frontier across our entire AI stack to drive new value for our customers and partners.”

SA contributor Jonathan Weber was also bullish in an analysis ahead of earnings, expecting “robust growth and a strong track record of outperforming analyst expectations.”

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 18 upward revisions and 6 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 21 upward revisions and 15 downward.
